Test Description: Validate negative-edge triggered GPIO interrupt behavior across GPIO pads [8..39] by enabling per-pin group interrupts, generating a falling edge per pad via a shared control register write, servicing the interrupt in Default_IRQHandler, verifying per-pin and group status bits, and clearing raw status before proceeding to the next pad.
Remarks: Build-time macro selects instance and IRQ: GPIO0 -> GIC 87 and SYSREG GPIO0 bits; GPIO1 -> GIC 88 and SYSREG GPIO1 bits. Uses external flag int_pend cleared in ISR. Relies on Default_IRQHandler being the active ISR.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry: test_case()
1) Initialize: test_err = 0.
2) If compiled with GPIO0: GIC_EnableIRQ(87); write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR).
3) If compiled with GPIO1: GIC_EnableIRQ(88); write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR).
4) Drive shared control register high: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff).
5) Configure pads 8..39 (32 pads): for (i = 0..31)
   5.1) addr1 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4).
   5.2) write_reg(addr1, (1u << 20) | (1u << 18) | (1u << 16)).
   5.3) wait_on(10).
6) For each pad index i = 0..31 execute:
   6.1) wr_val = (1u << i).
   6.2) Clear any pending raw status bit: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, wr_val).
   6.3) Enable per-group interrupt mask: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, wr_val).
   6.4) wait_on(10).
   6.5) Set software pending flag: int_pend = 1.
   6.6) Precondition drive: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff).
   6.7) wait_on(30).
   6.8) Generate falling edge on targeted pad: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, ~wr_val).
   6.9) Wait for ISR with timeout:
        - timeout = 5000.
        - while (int_pend && timeout--) { wait_on(10); }
        - if (timeout == 0) { printf timeout error for pad (i+8); test_err++; }
7) Complete: finish(test_err).

Interrupt Service: Default_IRQHandler()
A) local_wr = (1u << i).
B) Clear software pending: int_pend = 0.
C) Drive shared control high: write_reg(0xA0243ffc, 0xffffffff).
D) Read per-pad control/status: raddr = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4); rdata = read_reg(raddr).
E) If ((rdata & 0x1) != 0) then test_err++ (unexpected bit0 set).
F) If ((rdata & 0x2) != 0x0) then:
   F.1) rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1).
   F.2) If ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0) then test_err++ (expected pad bit not set in group status).
   F.3) Reprogram per-pad control (clear edge bit 18, keep bits 20 and 16): write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4), (1u << 20) | (1u << 16)).
   F.4) Clear raw interrupt status bit: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr).
   F.5) Verify group status cleared: rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1); if (rdata_grp != 0x0) test_err++.
   F.6) If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(87).
        If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(88).
G) Else (if (rdata & 0x2) == 0x0): test_err++ (expected edge status bit not set).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- No timeout occurs for any pad.
- In ISR: (rdata & 0x1) == 0; (rdata & 0x2) != 0; (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 & local_wr) != 0 on entry; after clearing raw status, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1 == 0x0; final finish(test_err) == 0.
Gap Analysis:
- No negative tests (e.g., interrupt disabled path or spurious interrupt conditions are not exercised).
- Limited error handling: failures increment test_err but execution proceeds; no recovery actions beyond status clears.
